name: FrootAI Enterprise Edition
description: Enterprise features for organizations deploying FrootAI at scale — private marketplace, custom agents, SSO, governance, air-gapped support

private_marketplace.description: Organization's own plugin/agent catalog — not public, accessible only to org members

implementation.frontend: Azure Static Web Apps with Entra ID auth
implementation.backend: Azure Cosmos DB for catalog, Azure Blob Storage for packages
implementation.api: REST API for CRUD on primitives, plays, plugins

capabilities: Publish private agents visible only within org
Private play catalog with org-specific customizations
Usage analytics per primitive (which teams use what)
Dependency tracking (which plays use which primitives)

sso_integration.description: Azure Entra ID, Okta, Auth0 for marketplace and tool access

usage_dashboard.description: Per-team token usage, cost attribution, quality metrics, primitive adoption
metrics: Token usage per team/user/play (daily, weekly, monthly)
Cost attribution by team, play, and model
Quality scores per play deployment (evaluation results over time)
Primitive adoption: which agents/skills/instructions are most used
Error rates and latency trends per play
alerts: Budget threshold exceeded (per team, per play)

governance_controls.description: Approve agents before org-wide deployment, policy enforcement, compliance gates
capabilities: Approval workflow: draft → review → approved → published
Policy enforcement via Azure Policy custom definitions
Mandatory evaluation gate before production deployment
Content safety verification on all agent outputs
Dependency approval: new Azure services require architecture review
Audit trail for all governance decisions

custom_agent_registry.description: Private agents visible only within org — Azure Container Registry for packages

implementation.registry: Azure Container Registry (ACR) for agent packages
implementation.testing: Automated validation on publish (npm run validate:primitives)

air_gapped_support.description: On-premises deployment without internet access for classified environments

implementation.deployment: Docker Compose bundle with all dependencies included
implementation.model_serving: Local model serving via Ollama or vLLM
implementation.sync: Periodic sync scheduler — USB/network transfer of updates

requirements: Minimum 16 GB RAM for local model serving
No internet required after initial setup

priority_support.description: Dedicated support channel, SLA guarantees, office hours

revenue_model.tertiary: Consulting services (architecture review, custom play development)
revenue_model.community_commitment: MIT core will always be free — enterprise features are add-ons, not paywalls
